Dustdevil  

Description

1: Decrease Hitpoints by 582.

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 162 Skill: Evocation
Casting Time: 5 Recast Time: 1.5
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Resist: Magic
Range: 200 Location: Any
Time of Day: Any Deletable: Yes
Reflectable: Yes Focusable: Yes
Dispellable: Yes Interruptable: Yes
Target Type: Single Spell Type: Detrimental
Source: Live 12/03

Messages

Cast on you: Debris smashes against your body.
Cast on other: Soandso 's body is crushed by flying debris.

Game Description

Ignites your target's skin, doing between 582 and @1 damage.

Items with this effect

  • Aged Smoldering Cudgel
  • Spell: Dustdevil

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Spell: Dustdevil
ClassLevel
RNG59
DRU43
Expansion:

Duration:
Instant
  • Comments (19)
  • Sold By (9)
  • NPCs with Ability (21)
  • Screenshots
Add Image

  • a grove scout

Merchants:


Abysmal Sea
  • Burumy Fahon - 30pp 7gp 8sp 1cp
  • Dadein Shadowbow - 30pp 7gp 8sp 1cp

East Freeport
  • Ardrian Zhusius - 10pp 4gp 9sp 9cp
  • Ennarn Shotius - 30pp 7gp 8sp 1cp

Firiona Vie
  • Arial Fern - 30pp 7gp 8sp 1cp
  • Raciel Sanherb - 36pp 6gp 4sp 5cp

The Overthere
  • Nyxaria T`Korin - 30pp 7gp 8sp 1cp

The Plane of Knowledge
  • Pathfinder Thoajin - 30pp 7gp 8sp 1cp
  • Wanderer Qenda - 30pp 7gp 8sp 1cp
Post Comment
Wrong Spell Icon
QuoteReply
# Dec 28 2006 at 5:20 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1618186627876
Scholar
104 posts
The spell icon (tornado) and name (Dustdevil) for this spell are more akin to the wind (stun) line of druid spells. However the spell description says Ignites for such and such, with no stun, which is more akin to the Fire line of druid spells. The damage to mana ratio seems to be better than L38 Firestrike, L48 Fire, and L48 Starfire. The save is magic for this spell not fire. Seems the tornado symbol is an error that was never fixed or that this spell was originally going to be another wind(stun) but got changed and vice versa, etc.

Edited, Dec 28th 2006 8:21pm by Harmoniq

Edited, Dec 28th 2006 8:23pm by Harmoniq

Edited, Dec 29th 2006 8:37pm by Harmoniq

# Sep 24 2005 at 2:33 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593374729465
Scholar
167 posts
I just found this spell in Plane of Knowledge (POK) from Pathfinder Viliken. Sells for 40p, 9g, 4 s, 9 cp.(bring 41 plat hehe).

It is listed as "Dustdevil".

Beats having to go to FV for it if you are already in POK.

Stun effect
QuoteReply
# May 12 2005 at 6:43 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592257266529
10 posts
Rangers dont need stuns. Load your procs, equip a sword of the bloodsworn and copper hammer....then pray. Stuns aren't for your class.
▲ Page top
Stun effect
QuoteReply
# Dec 07 2007 at 1:59 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1596384933676
Scholar
27 posts
If you really thing Rangers have no place using stuns, you're sadly mistaken. We've got a full line of self-buff proc spells that all have stuns, and they're an important part of every rangers agro management toolbox.

Play a ranger before you post about 'em.
▲ Page top
RE: Stun effect
QuoteReply
# Jun 01 2005 at 1:11 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592792149171
Scholar
44 posts
This does not seem like a good place for this type of thread, but since it is here... I have a ranger with a copper hammer and sword of the bloodsworn. Even with my spell procs I am able to manage agro and avoid stealing agro from tanks. In some situations however, being second or third on the hate list is a good thing. When that situation arises most clerics and/or enchanters usually appreciate my ability to grab agro when I need to.

# Apr 20 2005 at 5:37 PM Rating: Decent

Just purchased this in Firiona Vie as "Calification" but scribed as "Dust Devil"
▲ Page top
Calefaction vs Dustdevil?
QuoteReply
# Apr 14 2005 at 6:43 PM Rating: Decent
Sukrasisx
Scholar
463 posts
I was trying to check my spells to see what changed in the revamp this week, and was startled to find Calefaction is gone! This spell appears to have shown up in its place. (I've already moved on to Starfire, so I didn't have Calefaction in any spell sets, so I'm not really sure if Dustdevil is in its spot.) But since there are comments on this page dating back to Jan 2004, I gather Dustdevil is an old spell? Did they just throw it to us druids as compensation for losing Calefaction, or what?

Dustdevil looks like just another nuke, a bit smaller and cheaper than Starfire, part of a typical progression as you level up. Calefaction actually served a very different purpose: it was FAST (2 second cast), but with a long refresh (25? 30?). So you could use it when getting the damage 3-4 seconds sooner mattered, or if you were afraid a longer cast would get interrupted, but you couldn't use it for continuous damage.
____________________________
Sukrasisx (105 monk) / Terrwini (67 druid) / Teviron (59 shadowknight) / Wizbeau (36 wizard)
-- Mystic Coercion, EMarr
▲ Page top
Calefaction vs Dustdevil?
QuoteReply
# Apr 25 2025 at 6:50 PM Rating: Decent
ReliccInVisus
7 posts
I was looking at the spell list and knew calefaction was missing, went to investigate and it turns out they took it out.. Wow. You are right it was useful

great
QuoteReply
# Jan 02 2004 at 10:54 AM Rating: Decent
dafdaf
View User Forum
Scholar
1,273 posts
I personally love this spell. When I'm made to be MT it's great to claim a lot of agro early on in the fight; when just meleeing it's great for doing an additional 900 damage; and when soloing, it means the mobs (for once) don't laugh when we through magic at em!
